This structure translates to a global implication `G(HELLO_MSG_SENT -> CHAIN_OF_OPERATIONS_HAPPENED_BEFORE)`. First, 'an HELLO message is exchanged' refers to the Local Node sending an HELLO message, which maps to `hello_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi`. Second, 'this chain of operations' is detailed as 'Local Node—before generating the message—must query the Link Set, Neighbor Set, and MPR Set components'. This translates to the conjunction `(local_node_queried_link_set & local_node_queried_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_mpr_set)`. Let's call this conjunction `QUERIES`. The temporal relationship 'without this chain of operations' implies that `QUERIES` must have occurred at some point before the `hello_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi`. The LTL pattern for 'P must happen before Q' is `!((!P) U Q)`. Applying this, 'QUERIES happened before HELLO_MSG_SENT' becomes `!((!QUERIES) U hello_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi)`. Combining these parts, the final formula is `G(hello_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi -> !((!(local_node_queried_link_set & local_node_queried_neighbor_set & local_node_queried_mpr_set)) U hello_msg_sent_local_node_to_dmi))`.
